channel.title: Comments for page "I Don't Wanna Be Special (I Just Wanna Survive)"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "I Don't Wanna Be Special (I Just Wanna Survive)"

item.title: amazing read
item.content:encoded: god I loved this tale so much, for the first time ever I actually felt attached to the protagonist of a tale!!! And it does it all whilst barely even mentioning anything to do with SCP and I think that makes it all the better knowing that this character and his actions and his whole world is taking place in the same world as the entire SCP foundation is enough to be honest. A VERY solid upvote

item.title: Re: dislike
item.content:encoded:
The poster only wanted to say that there is a lack of characterisation of Liv and father, that they're too stereotypical and lacks anything that makes them stand out from the rest of the characters from other YA novels this takes inspiration from. That it has the novel idea of writing about LGBT 'realistically' but fails in its execution.

This is completely valid criticism. Where did aeternam say they were uncomfortable with the heavy LGBT theme? What part of their statement mentioned that they disliked it because of the LGBT theme? It's not thinly-veiled. They're clearly expressing their dislike for the characters, not the coming-of-age coming-out-the-closet story Oboe is tryna tell.

Please think carefully before accusing others of dissing the LGBT theme just because you don't agree with their opinions.

item.title: Re: dislike
item.content:encoded: This really seems like a thinly-veiled attempt at justifying disliking this piece just because of the heavy LGBT themes. If you'd just downvoted without saying anything, you wouldn'tve broadcast to the entire world how you feel uncomfortable with stories with gay people in them.
Well, what can I say? This is fantastic.

item.title: Re: dislike
item.content:encoded: Which gaw article are you talking about?

item.title: dislike
item.content:encoded:
Sorry but I really disliked it. It is very stereotypical, the dad has no personnality he is the common asshole homophobic. The mom barely exist, Liv seems to be a pretext in the story. Only the males characters have some personnality. I understand that this is supposed to be LGBT friendly but it lack a really story.
The female character are useless and the dad is just the homophobic scarecrow. I understand that you need him for the story but make him human and relatable and it will be efficient same for every girls in this story.
It feel very long, it feel like this teenage serie you have already seen. It lack personnality.
I m sorry for the harsh criticism, I think that having 60+ like you don't really care, but I feel like the idea of making an LGBT friendly story is nice but not enought, it need to be written like a tale not like a moral lesson.
For lgbt story there is gamers against weed even if I sometimes find them over the top they are very well written.
If I skipped a major reference to something you can tell me, I m still open about it
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow.

Thank you for sharing this in IRC earlier. I really feel this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was an absolutely fantastic read. Eric's reactions to everything around him feels so organic and real. Reading it all was like watching SLC Punk for the first time. Eric's total blindness to his own magic was also a fun inclusion.

I can't sing many praises that haven't already been sung. I really liked this tale and I really liked Eric.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I told you how exceptional this was when I read it but I'll say it again - this is one of the most raw and realistic takes on the teenage experience I've read not only on this site but overall - and I read a lot of YA. My heart broke for Eric so sharply and into so many pieces because I felt like I knew him. You did that in this small space and it's magnificent. One of my favorite tales on site. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love everything about this. The story-telling and characterization are done incredibly and the overall message throughout is amazing. Excellent work.
Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this thing so goddamn much you seriously have no idea I'm so happy that you wrote something based on my canon like ;-; <3 this tale made my heart feel so many things? this is amazing

a trillion +1's from me!

and you did it you linked BMC you officially succeed at life this gave me so many BMC vibes
This is one of the best tales I have ever read on this site. I can't begin to express how great of a relatable emotional roller coaster this story is, and I wish I could upvote it a thousand times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bi rights. And for the rest, still love the story. Was really into it, super emotional, super relatable, super accurate, super everything. I think I definitely love this new stuff about Eric.

+2000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t wanna be a hero, just wanna stay in the line
I’ll never be a Rob De Niro, for me Joe Pesci is fine
And so I follow my own rules and I use them as my tools
To stay alive
I don’t wanna be special, no, no
I just wanna survive


—More Than Survive (Be More Chill)

Yes I named this tale after a Be More Chill song don’t @ me we all knew this would happen

Wow, it feels good to post this. I’ve had this tale in the works for a good couple of months now, like almost a year or something? The thought came to me after reading Mew-ltiverse and not_a_seagull's collab con article Eric's Journal and realizing applying the same principle to an older Eric article might yield some interesting results.

Thanks to my critters: Uncle Nicolini, barredowl, DrAkimoto, The Pighead, AbsentmindedNihilist, and especially to Mew-ltiverse who encouraged me to make this and for looking at, brainstorming, and critting this article over the nine months or so I've been working on it.

If you liked this, consider checking out some of my other articles.
